sections.paragraph:
i.q. Arab. هَانَ to be light, easy, compare cogn. אוּן, אִין. Hence-

(1) to be of little moment. Arab. Conj. II. IV. X. to esteem of little worth, to contemn. So Hiphil, Deu 1:41, וַתָּהִינוּ לַעֲלֹות “ye thought it but little to go up,” i.e. ye acted lightly and rashly in that ye went up. Others take it “contemning (the command of God) ye went up.” Comp. Num 14:44.

(2) to be in easy circumstances, to live comfortably, to be rich. Compare אוּן No. 2, 3. Hence are derived הִין, and-
